sourceMovie.title: Before Sunset
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Before Sunset for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Richard Linklater keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Notebook
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Before Sunset clicked for you, The Notebook (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Before Sunset?
faq.answer: Try The Notebook, Moulin Rouge!, Before Sunrise—each shares DNA with Before Sunset while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Before Sunset?
faq.answer: Before Sunset (2004) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Before Sunset part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Before... Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
